seo.title: Building Workflow Automation with Agno + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build workflow automation with Agno + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building Workflow Automation with Agno enables multi-step AI agents to handle complex tasks like API integrations, conditional branching, and human approvals with lightweight, model-agnostic design. Guardrails are essential to prevent costly errors in production, such as invalid API calls, permission violations, or failed rollbacks that could disrupt business processes. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure action correctness and reliability at scale.

architectureOverview.heading: Workflow Automation Architecture with Agno
architectureOverview.description:
Agno agents orchestrate multi-step workflows by combining tools, memory, and structured outputs. A typical architecture starts with a PlannerAgent that decomposes user requests into executable steps, followed by WorkerAgents handling API calls, data validation, and conditional logic. Human-in-the-loop steps pause execution for approval via webhooks or email notifications, with persistent memory tracking state across sessions.

The workflow integrates external APIs (CRM, payment gateways) through custom tools, uses structured Pydantic models for output validation, and supports multimodal inputs for document processing. Rollback mechanisms are implemented via transaction logs, ensuring idempotency even after failures. Agno's session management maintains context across retries and human interventions.

Vex wraps critical agent.run() calls to verify actions before execution, catching issues like incorrect API payloads or missing permissions in real-time.

risks.name: Invalid API Payloads
risks.description: Agent generates incorrect structured data for external API calls, causing 4xx errors
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies action payloads against API schemas before execution

risks.name: Permission Escalation
risks.description: Agent attempts unauthorized API actions beyond configured scopes
risks.mitigation: Vex validates permissions and required scopes for each tool call

risks.name: Non-Idempotent Execution
risks.description: Duplicate workflow runs create multiple charges or data entries
risks.mitigation: Vex checks idempotency keys and transaction states before proceeding

risks.name: Failed Rollback Detection
risks.description: Partial failures leave system in inconsistent state without proper cleanup
risks.mitigation: Vex monitors rollback execution and verifies final system state

implementation.title: Basic Workflow Agent Setup
implementation.description: Create a multi-step workflow agent with API tools and structured output
implementation.code:
from agno.agent import Agent
from agno.tools.apis import ApiTool
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field

class PaymentRequest(BaseModel):
    amount: float = Field(..., gt=0)
    customer_id: str
    idempotency_key: str

payment_tool = ApiTool(
    name="process_payment",
    description="Process payment via Stripe API",
    url="https://api.stripe.com/v1/charges",
    method="POST",
    auth_type="bearer"
)

workflow_agent = Agent(
    name="WorkflowAgent",
    model="gpt-4o",
    tools=[payment_tool],
    output_model=PaymentRequest,
    instructions="Execute payment workflows safely with idempotency"
)

implementation.title: Conditional Logic Implementation
implementation.description: Add branching logic based on API responses and human approval
implementation.code:
from agno.agent import Agent
from agno.tools import Tool

class ApprovalTool(Tool):
    name = "request_human_approval"
    description = "Request human approval for high-value transactions"
    
    def run(self, amount: float, action: str) -> str:
        if amount > 1000:
            return "PENDING_APPROVAL"
        return "APPROVED"

approval_tool = ApprovalTool()

workflow_agent = Agent(
    name="ConditionalWorkflow",
    tools=[payment_tool, approval_tool],
    instructions="""Check approval requirements before processing payments.
    For amounts > $1000, request human approval first."""
)

result = workflow_agent.run("Process $1500 payment for customer123")

implementation.title: Idempotency and Rollback
implementation.description: Implement transaction tracking for safe retries and cleanup

implementation.title: Multi-Step Orchestration
implementation.description: Orchestrate complete workflow with error handling and state management
implementation.code:
def execute_workflow(customer_id: str, amount: float):
    try:
        # Step 1: Create transaction
        tx_id = tx_manager.create_transaction(customer_id)
        
        # Step 2: Agent execution with structured output
        result = workflow_agent.run(
            f"Process payment of ${amount} for {customer_id}. "
            f"Transaction ID: {tx_id}"
        )
        
        # Step 3: Verify and commit
        if result.status == "success":
            tx_manager.transactions[tx_id]['status'] = 'completed'
            return result
        else:
            tx_manager.rollback(tx_id)
            raise Exception("Workflow failed")
            
    except Exception as e:
        tx_manager.rollback(tx_id)
        raise

# Usage
result = execute_workflow("customer123", 1500.00)

vexIntegration.title: Verify Action Correctness
vexIntegration.description: Wrap agent.run() to validate API payloads before execution
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import verify

@verify(
    checks=[
        "action_correctness",
        "structured_output_valid"
    ],
    risk_profile="medium"
)
def safe_workflow_execution(customer_id: str, amount: float):
    result = workflow_agent.run(
        f"Process ${amount} payment for {customer_id}"
    )
    return result

# Vex automatically validates:
# - Correct tool selection
# - Valid structured output
# - Expected API payload format
verified_result = safe_workflow_execution("customer123", 1500.00)

vexIntegration.title: Permission Validation
vexIntegration.description: Ensure agent only calls authorized APIs with correct scopes

vexIntegration.title: Idempotency and Rollback Verification
vexIntegration.description: Verify transaction state and ensure safe rollback execution

cta.heading: Secure Your Agno Workflow Automation
cta.description: Add Vex verification to your Agno workflows today with pip install vex-sdk to prevent production failures.
